name: Baked Cinnamon-Applesauce Doughnuts
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in warm water in a large bowl; let stand for 5 minutes.
2. Add 3 cups flour, applesauce, and the next six ingredients; beat at low speed of an electric mixer until moistened.
3. Beat at medium speed for an additional 2 minutes.
4. Stir in 2 cups of flour, 1/2 cup at a time, to form a soft dough.
5. Turn out onto a well-floured surface. Knead until smooth and elastic (about 5 minutes); add enough remaining flour, 1 tablespoon at a time, to prevent the dough from sticking to hands.
6. Place dough in a bowl coated with cooking spray, turning to coat top. Cover and let rise in a warm place, free from drafts, for 1 hour or until doubled in bulk.
7. Punch dough down, and turn out onto a lightly floured surface. Roll dough to 1/2 inch thickness; cut with a lightly floured 2-1/2 inch doughnut cutter.
8. Combine doughnut holes and any remaining scraps of dough; reroll to 1/2 inch thickness and cut as before.
9. Place doughnuts on baking sheets coated with cooking spray. Brush 2 tablespoons melted margarine over doughnuts.
10. Let rise, uncovered, in a warm, draft-free place for 40 minutes.
11. Combine 1/2 cup sugar and 1 teaspoon cinnamon in a large zip-top heavy-duty plastic bag.
12. Bake doughnuts at 425 degrees for 8 minutes or until golden.
13. Immediately brush remaining 2 tablespoons melted margarine over baked doughnuts; add doughnuts to plastic bag.
14. Seal the bag and shake to coat.
15. Yield: 3 dozen doughnuts
